glazes.name: Discontinued Basic White
glazes.description: SP1 - Basic White An opaque semi matte white that becomes less opaque at higher firing temps

glazes.name: Sand
glazes.description: SP2 - Sand CONE 4 - 6 Glossy, opaque, creamy white, with semi-dense black peppering throughout. Breaks over texture leaving speckles. This glaze can be a bit "fluxy", meaning it can run. Leave room at the bottom of your pieces or apply in lighter coats. Even better . use it on top of other glazes to see some amazing results! .

glazes.name: Green Mottled
glazes.description: SP4 - Green Mottled A semi opaque blue-green glaze with a mottled surface full of deep green splotches

glazes.name: Light Blue Mottled
glazes.description: SP5 - Light Blue Mottled An opaque blue mottled glaze with blues ranging from deep cobalt to a pure robin's egg blue

glazes.name: Brown Mottled
glazes.description: SP6 - Brown Mottled A semi-transparent amber glaze with a tan mottled surface and rust highlights. Full Safety Data Sheet is available upon request. Glaze in wet form poses no or low health risk and no inhalation risk. Once glaze has dried, there may be dust generated by cleaning and working processes. In the event that dust is generated, use local exhaust ventilation or other engineering controls as required to maintain exposures below applicable occupational exposure limits (TLV). Do not dispo

glazes.name: Yellow Stain
glazes.description: SP7 - Yellow Stain Beautiful sunshine yellow with a light white break. Even opaque coverage when applied with 3 layers. FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 .

glazes.name: Opaque White
glazes.description: SP8 - Opaque White For CONE 0 4 - 6 A glossy opaque white glaze. An all-time favorite, go-to white glaze. This glaze can be flux-y when used heavily, watch your bottoms and test when using in combination. ___________ .

glazes.name: Gray Blue Mottled
glazes.description: SP10 - Gray Blue Mottled A grey blue mottled glaze that breaks into a transparent olive green

glazes.name: Ochre
glazes.description: SP11 - Ochre Fires to CONE 0 4 - 6 A glossy deep brown flux-y glaze with tan mottled surface. Breaks into a transparent chocolate brown and floats beautifully over other glazes. On it's own you can see some amazing green and blues being pulled out from under the darker surface. Note: this is a very fluid glaze and will run if applied too thickly. Leave at least a 1/2" space at the bottom of pieces for potential running. Use moderation for best results! ___________ .

glazes.name: Molasses
glazes.description: SP12 - Molasses FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 A deep, luscious brown with the rich, syrupy goodness of its namesake. This glaze creates a warm, organic (can we say a little mid-century retro?) finish that’s ideal for rustic ware or accent pieces

glazes.name: Black Stain
glazes.description: SP13 - Black Stain A deep black glaze with a blue mottled surface. Full Safety Data Sheet is available upon request. Glaze in wet form poses no or low health risk and no inhalation risk. Once glaze has dried, there may be dust generated by cleaning and working processes. In the event that dust is generated, use local exhaust ventilation or other engineering controls as required to maintain exposures below applicable occupational exposure limits (TLV). Do not dispose of into sinks or toilets. Nev

glazes.name: Turquoise
glazes.description: SP15 - Turquoise Turquoise is one of our favorite glazes for obvious reasons (the glorious bright color). It goes on seamlessly with a brush, breaks nicely on the edges, and combines with just about everything. And, oh! again, the color. Note: this is a very fluid glaze and will run if applied too thickly. Use moderation for best results! _______ .

glazes.name: Burnt Orange
glazes.description: SP17 - Burnt Orange For CONE 0 4 - 6 A toasty orange-red glaze that can get semi opaque where thick

glazes.name: Turquoise Green
glazes.description: SP18 - Turquoise Green For CONE 0 4 - 6 A lime green glaze that pools into cracks as a mottled turquoise blue. Breaks translucent green

glazes.name: Stormy Gray
glazes.description: SP19 - Stormy Gray A deep brown glaze with a slightly iridescent mottled surface

images.alt: A square Seattle Pottery Supply glaze example tile in a rich, speckled purple gray with some breaks. This is a sample of SP19 Stormy Gray midrange pottery glaze

glazes.name: Chrome Green
glazes.description: SP22 - Chrome Green Fires to CONE 0 4 - 6 An opaque chrome green glaze that breaks to golden-tan .

glazes.name: Deep Crystal Blue
glazes.description: SP24 - Deep Crystal Blue A deep cobalt blue that breaks to a pale bluish white

glazes.name: Dark Blue Mottled
glazes.description: SP25 - Dark Blue Mottled A deep cobalt blue midrange glaze, with a lighter blue mottled surface

glazes.name: Peacock
glazes.description: SP27 - Peacock A beautiful bold teal glaze that shines on most clay bodies. The opacity of this glaze is complimented with lighter sea green mottling, highlighting texture of the piece beneath

glazes.name: Clear
glazes.description: SP29 - Clear, Zinc-free For CONE 4 - 6 A zinc-free midrange clear glaze. SPS glazes are made in small batches and with extreme attention to quality control. Lead free and safe for dinnerware

glazes.name: Speckled Ivory
glazes.description: SP31 - Speckled Ivory For CONE 4 - 6 Creamy and inviting with natural, speckled variation. A studio staple for layering or letting its subtle complexity shine on its own

glazes.name: White Satin
glazes.description: SP32 - White Satin Fires to CONE 4 - 6 An opaque glossy white glaze with subtle red speckles. Layers beautifully with other glazes

glazes.name: Blue Satin
glazes.description: SP34 - Blue Satin For CONE 4 - 6 A silky, soft-touch blue with a gentle sheen that breaks to a pale chartreuse—a bit like the sky after the clouds clear. This satin finish glaze serves a serene, sophisticated calm

glazes.name: Black Satin
glazes.description: SP35 - Black Satin A deep glossy black that breaks brown

glazes.name: Green Satin
glazes.description: SP36 - Green Satin For CONE 4 - 6 Organic and leafy, this smooth green satin glaze brings nature’s tones to your work with a lovely soft green that breaks tan. Excellent layering potential

glazes.name: Turquoise Matte
glazes.description: SP37 - Turquoise Matte For CONE 4 - 6 A semi-opaque and satiny deep turquoise green glaze. This product will darken and create a slight crystallization where it pools. This glaze does have a tendency to crawl so make sure not to apply too heavily! This glaze does not pass our leeching test and should only be used for decorative or exterior surfaces that do not interact directly with food

glazes.name: White Matte
glazes.description: SP41 - White Matte A matte white glaze that breaks to the color of the clay underneath

glazes.name: Gold Rust
glazes.description: SP44 - Gold Rust FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 A bright yellow gold speckled glaze. SP44 Gold Rust produces dark red-bronze speckles that sit over an opaque surface. This glaze combines well with other glazes, creating some beautiful mottling effects

glazes.name: Moss Brown
glazes.description: SP50 - Moss Brown For CONE 4 - 6 A matte earthy brown with a deep brown, rust, and yellow mottled surface

glazes.name: Moss Black
glazes.description: SP51 - Moss Black FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 A moody, textural black with mossy undertones that shift with thickness and firing. A versatile glaze for bold forms and dramatic contrast

glazes.name: Shadow Green
glazes.description: SP52 - Shadow Green FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 SPS glazes are made in small batches and with extreme attention to quality control. Lead free and safe for dinnerware

glazes.name: Oatmeal Rust
glazes.description: SP55 - Oatmeal Rust FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 A gorgeous tan-speckled glaze. Provides an even opaque surface on its own, and layered? Oh, it blends like a dream

glazes.name: Metallic Brown
glazes.description: SP59 - Metallic Brown Fires to CONE 4 - 6 Rich and reflective, this glaze creates a metallic sheen over a rich, warm red-brown base, perfect for statement pieces and experimental surfaces

glazes.name: Gold Satin Metallic
glazes.description: FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 ~ 10% OFF THROUGH THE END OF THE YEAR! ~ Celebrate our Golden Anniversary (50 years!) with 10% off our favorite glitzy-gold metallic glaze. A rich, burnished dark gold, Gold Satin Metallic is great for high-impact glaze effects (and fun to mix with other glazes, too). The discount will be automatically applied in your cart. ______ .

images.alt: SP-60 Gold Satin Metallic glaze over SPS Fool's Gold pottery clay. Work by Frank Jacques.

glazes.name: Sky Blue
glazes.description: SP202 - Sky Blue A glossy pale blue glaze with a light crackle

glazes.name: Light Lavender
glazes.description: SP204 - Light Lavender For CONE 4 - 6 A glossy light purple glaze

glazes.name: Celedon
glazes.description: SP206 - Celedon For CONE 4 - 6 A glossy pale green semitransparent glaze

glazes.name: Yellow
glazes.description: SP209 - Yellow A glossy, opaque bright yellow glaze

glazes.name: Orange
glazes.description: SP210 - Orange For CONE 4 - 6 A semi-transparent glossy highlighter orange. Sunglasses, anyone? .

glazes.name: Salmon
glazes.description: SP211 - Salmon For CONE 4 - 6 A glossy deep burnt orange glaze with a touch of pink

glazes.name: Tomato Red
glazes.description: SP212 - Tomato Red FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 .

glazes.name: Black
glazes.description: SP215 - Black FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 Solid Black Glaze. Great for lining interiors or combinations with other glazes. Full Safety Data Sheet is available upon request. Glaze in wet form poses no or low health risk and no inhalation risk. Once glaze has dried, there may be dust generated by cleaning and working processes. In the event that dust is generated, use local exhaust ventilation or other engineering controls as required to maintain exposures below applicable occupational exposure limits

glazes.name: Purple
glazes.description: SP216 - Purple For CONE 4 - 6 Opaque purple that deepens to a plum where thick. Slight breakage over texture. Fades beautifully over darker clay bodies, lightening to a dustier periwinkle color. Still allow speckles from clay body to show through

glazes.name: White
glazes.description: SP217 - White FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 Beautifully basic white. Opaque while still allowing speckles and texture shine through. Perfect for mug liners and sanitary ware

glazes.name: Burgundy
glazes.description: SP218 - Burgundy FIRING RANGE CONE 4 - 6 A beautiful merlot colored glaze. Breaks white and pools into a deep plum color. Use 3-5 coats for maximum depth and consistency

glazes.name: Mossy Green
glazes.description: SP222 - Mossy Green Mossy Green is one of our favorite glazes. The glaze equivalent of a mossy rock near a shaded forest pool, it provides a deep mottled surface of brown, green, and a bit of blue. Totally gorgeous. ____ .

glazes.name: Lichen Green
glazes.description: SP223 - Lichen Green For CONE 4 - 6 If you like organic effects, this glaze is for you. A deep army green with a mottled surface of browns, Lichen Green really does have the feel of lichen growing on a rock. Dark and a bit moody, but oh-so-wild. What's not to love? ___ .
